case_title: GEORGE E. SANTOS COLORADO VS. THE BANK OF MELLON NEW YORK et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON, FKA THE BANK OF NEW YORK'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. THE MOTION SHOULD BE RE-NOTICED FOR HEARING IN DEPARTMENT 501. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 = (302/PJB)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF MAY-01-2013 CONTINUED TO JUN-12-2013 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO SEPARATE CAUSES OF ACTION. TO THE EXTENT COMPLAINT SEEKS TO ENFORCE MECHANIC'S LIEN, DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE, IN GOOD FAITH, EXTENSION OF CREDIT PURSUANT TO CIVIL CODE 8460(B); IF SUCH AMENDMENT CANNOT BE MADE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS COMPLAINT WAS NOT FILED WITHIN 90 DAYS OF RECORDATION OF LIEN. MOVING PARTY IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.6C IN FUTURE. PREVAILING PARTY PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE A JAMES ROBERTSON, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Sustained with leave to amend to separate causes of action. To the extent Complaint seeks to enforce mechanic's lien, Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to allege, in good faith, extension of credit pursuant to Civil Code 8460(b); if such amendment cannot be made demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as complaint was not filed within 90 days of recordation of lien. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. Prevailing party prepare order. Judge A James Robertson, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D501)
